meaning: explain or be the reason for
example:
How do you account for the loss?
This accounts for the delay.
He couldn't account for the money.

meaning: support; or make a copy of data
example:
Back up your files.
I'll back you up.
Can someone back up this claim?

He blew up over a small thing.
The balloon blew up.
Don't blow up at me.

The reform brought about change.
What brought this about?
Technology brought about progress.

meaning: ask someone to do something; or visit
example:
They called on him to resign.
I'll call on you tomorrow.
She was called on to speak.

They carried out the tests.
Carry out the plan.
The work was carried out safely.

meaning: reach the same level as others
example:
I need to catch up on reading.
Let's catch up soon.
He caught up with the group.

meaning: think of an idea or plan
example:
We came up with a solution.
She came up with the name.
Can you come up with ideas?

You can count on me.
Don't count on it.
We count on your support.

They drew up a contract.
Draw up a list.
We drew up a budget.

meaning: take someone somewhere; or fall asleep
example:
I'll drop you off.
He dropped off during the film.
Sales dropped off.

We get by on a small income.
I can get by in French.
They just get by.

Let's get rid of the old sofa.
Get rid of the clutter.
I can't get rid of this cough.

meaning: give for free; or reveal a secret
example:
They gave away free samples.
Don't give away the ending.
She gave away her old clothes.

Hand in your work by Friday.
He handed in his notice.
Please hand it in.

meaning: stop yourself; or keep something secret
example:
Don't hold back.
She held back her tears.
He's holding back information.

We'll look into the problem.
Let me look into it.
Police are looking into the case.

meaning: see or hear with difficulty; or understand
example:
I couldn't make out the sign.
Can you make out the words?
I can't make out his writing.

Her grandfather passed away.
He passed away peacefully.
They lost a friend who passed away.

She put forward a proposal.
He put his name forward.
They put forward a new idea.

We can't rule out rain.
Don't rule it out.
Police ruled out foul play.

meaning: save for later; or ignore for now
example:
Set aside some money.
Let's set aside our differences.
She set aside time to study.

Her work stands out.
He stands out in a crowd.
One detail stood out.

She takes after her mother.
He takes after his dad.
Who do you take after?

It's a lot to take in.
I couldn't take it all in.
Don't be taken in by him.

He turned down the offer.
Turn down the music.
They turned my request down.

The rain turned into snow.
It turned into a great day.
The caterpillar turned into a butterfly.

Watch out for the step.
Watch out, it's hot!
Watch out for scams.

meaning: become unusable from use; or exhaust
example:
My shoes wore out.
The kids wore me out.
The batteries wore out.

They set up a charity.
We set up the equipment.
She set up her own business.

I'm coming down with a cold.
She came down with the flu.
He came down with a fever.
